WITH feb_customers AS (

total_customers AS (

    FROM feb_customers
),

    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT CustomerID) AS high_count
    FROM feb_customers
    WHERE Consumption > 528.3

1482:
WITH consumption_by_segment_year AS (
    SELECT 
        c.Segment,
        SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4) AS Year,
        SUM(ym.Consumption) AS TotalConsumption
    FROM yearmonth ym
    INNER JOIN customers c ON ym.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
    WHERE c.Currency = 'EUR'
        AND c.Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM')
        AND SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4) IN ('2012', '2013')
    GROUP BY c.Segment, SUBSTR(ym.Date, 1, 4)
),
pivot_consumption AS (
    SELECT 
        Segment,
        MAX(CASE WHEN Year = '2012' THEN TotalConsumption END) AS consumption_2012,
        MAX(CASE WHEN Year = '2013' THEN TotalConsumption END) AS consumption_2013
    FROM consumption_by_segment_year
    GROUP BY Segment
),
percentage_change AS (
    SELECT 
        Segment,
        consumption_2012,
        consumption_2013,
        (consumption_2013 - consumption_2012) AS increase,
        CASE 
            WHEN consumption_2013 > 0 
            THEN ((consumption_2013 - consumption_2012) / consumption_2013) * 100.0
            ELSE NULL 
        END AS percentage_increase
    FROM pivot_consumption
    WHERE consumption_2012 IS NOT NULL 
        AND consumption_2013 IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    Segment,
    consumption_2012,
    consumption_2013,
    increase,
    percentage_increase
FROM percentage_change
ORDER BY percentage_increase DESC;

WITH customers_consumption_2013 AS (
    SELECT 
        c.CustomerID,
        c.Segment,
        SUM(ym.Consumption) AS total_consumption
    FROM customers c
    INNER JOIN yearmonth ym ON c.CustomerID = ym.CustomerID
    WHERE c.Currency = 'CZK'
        AND ym.Date BETWEEN '201301' AND '201312'
    GROUP BY c.CustomerID, c.Segment
),
segment_min_consumption AS (
    SELECT 
        Segment,
        MIN(total_consumption) AS min_consumption
    FROM customers_consumption_2013
    WHERE Segment IN ('SME', 'LAM', 'KAM')
    GROUP BY Segment
),
least_customers AS (
    SELECT 
        cc.CustomerID,
        cc.Segment,
        cc.total_consumption
    FROM customers_consumption_2013 cc
    INNER JOIN segment_min_consumption smc 
        ON cc.Segment = smc.Segment 
        AND cc.total_consumption = smc.min_consumption
),
segment_avg_consumption AS (
    SELECT 
        Segment,
        SUM(total_consumption) * 1.0 / COUNT(*) AS avg_consumption
    FROM least_customers
    GROUP BY Segment
),
averages AS (
    SELECT 
        MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'SME' THEN avg_consumption END) AS avg_sme,
        MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'LAM' THEN avg_consumption END) AS avg_lam,
        MAX(CASE WHEN Segment = 'KAM' THEN avg_consumption END) AS avg_kam
    FROM segment_avg_consumption
)
SELECT 
    avg_sme - avg_lam AS diff_SME_LAM,
    avg_lam - avg_kam AS diff_LAM_KAM,
    avg_kam - avg_sme AS diff_KAM_SME
FROM averages;

WITH target_customer AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT CustomerID
    FROM transactions_1k
    WHERE Date = '2012-08-25' AND Price = 634.8
),
consumption_summary AS (
    SELECT 
        y.CustomerID,
        SUM(CASE WHEN y.Date LIKE '2012%' THEN y.Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2012,
        SUM(CASE WHEN y.Date LIKE '2013%' THEN y.Consumption ELSE 0 END) AS consumption_2013
    FROM yearmonth y
    WHERE y.CustomerID IN (SELECT CustomerID FROM target_customer)
    GROUP BY y.CustomerID
)
SELECT 
    c.CustomerID,
    c.consumption_2012,
    c.consumption_2013,
    CASE 
        WHEN c.consumption_2012 > 0 
        THEN (c.consumption_2012 - c.consumption_2013) / c.consumption_2012 
        ELSE NULL 
    END AS consumption_decrease_rate
FROM consumption_summary c;

    SELECT member_id
    FROM member
    WHERE t_shirt_size = 'Medium' AND position = 'Member'
),
members_with_50 AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT m.member_id
    FROM medium_members m
    INNER JOIN income i ON m.member_id = i.link_to_member
    WHERE i.amount = 50
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM members_with_50) * 100.0 / 
    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM medium_members) AS percentage
FROM medium_members
LIMIT 1;

WITH male_counts AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(CASE WHEN Admission = '+' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS inpatient_male,
        SUM(CASE WHEN Admission = '-' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS outpatient_male
    FROM Patient
    WHERE SEX = 'M'
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN inpatient_male > outpatient_male THEN 'in-patient'
        WHEN outpatient_male > inpatient_male THEN 'outpatient'
        ELSE 'equal'
    END AS more_group,
    ROUND(
        (CAST(inpatient_male AS REAL) / NULLIF(outpatient_male, 0)) * 100, 
        2
    ) AS percentage_inpatient_to_outpatient
FROM male_counts;

WITH abnormal_patients AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT ID
    FROM Laboratory
    WHERE (PLT <= 100 OR PLT >= 400) AND PLT IS NOT NULL
),
patient_flags AS (
    SELECT 
        ap.ID,
        MAX(CASE WHEN l.PLT < 100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS had_low,
        MAX(CASE WHEN l.PLT > 400 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS had_high
    FROM abnormal_patients ap
    JOIN Laboratory l ON ap.ID = l.ID
    WHERE l.PLT IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY ap.ID
)
SELECT 
    SUM(had_low) AS patients_with_lower_platelet,
    SUM(had_high) AS patients_with_higher_platelet,
    SUM(had_low) - SUM(had_high) AS difference_lower_minus_higher
FROM patient_flags;

WITH height_extremes AS (
    SELECT MAX(height) AS max_height, MIN(height) AS min_height
    FROM Player
),
tallest_players AS (
    SELECT p.player_api_id
    FROM Player p, height_extremes
    WHERE p.height = height_extremes.max_height
),
shortest_players AS (
    SELECT p.player_api_id
    FROM Player p, height_extremes
    WHERE p.height = height_extremes.min_height
),
avg_finishing_tallest AS (
    SELECT AVG(pa.finishing) AS avg_finish
    FROM tallest_players tp
    JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON tp.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
    WHERE pa.finishing IS NOT NULL
),
avg_finishing_shortest AS (
    SELECT AVG(pa.finishing) AS avg_finish
    FROM shortest_players sp
    JOIN Player_Attributes pa ON sp.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id
    WHERE pa.finishing IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT 
    CASE 
        WHEN COALESCE(t.avg_finish, 0) >= COALESCE(s.avg_finish, 0) 
            THEN 'highest'
        ELSE 'shortest'
    END AS player_group,
    COALESCE(t.avg_finish, s.avg_finish) AS average_finishing_rate
FROM avg_finishing_tallest t
CROSS JOIN avg_finishing_shortest s
ORDER BY COALESCE(t.avg_finish, 0) >= COALESCE(s.avg_finish, 0) DESC
LIMIT 1;

WITH players_with_foot AS (
    SELECT 
        p.player_api_id,
        pa.preferred_foot
    FROM Player p
    INNER JOIN (
        SELECT 
            player_api_id,
            preferred_foot,
            ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY player_api_id ORDER BY date DESC) AS rn
        FROM Player_Attributes
        WHERE preferred_foot IS NOT NULL
    ) pa ON p.player_api_id = pa.player_api_id AND pa.rn = 1
    WHERE strftime('%Y', p.birthday) BETWEEN '1987' AND '1992'
)
SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_players,
    SUM(CASE WHEN preferred_foot = 'left' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS left_footed_players,
    (SUM(CASE WHEN preferred_foot = 'left' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_left_footed
FROM players_with_foot;

The champion is the driver with the maximum points at the last race (or the driver with position = 1 in the last race). We can find the last race of 2009 (max round) and then get driverId with position = 1 for that race. Alternatively, we can sum points over all races in 2009 and pick the driver with max sum. But the champion is determined by cumulative points, not sum of points per race? Actually, driverStandings points are cumulative after each race. So the last race's standings reflect the final championship standings. So we can get the driverId with position = 1 in the driverStandings for the last race of 2009.

2. Find the last race of 2009: select raceId from races where year=2009 order by round desc limit 1.

3. Get champion driverId from driverStandings where raceId = that raceId and position = 1.

4. Then, from results join races on raceId where year=2009 and driverId = champion driverId, and we have fastestLap and fastestLapTime. We need to find the row with the minimum fastestLapTime (converted to milliseconds). But fastestLapTime may be NULL for some races (if driver did not set a fastest lap). We need to ignore NULLs.

5. Convert fastestLapTime to milliseconds: we can use time in milliseconds? Actually, there is a column `milliseconds` in results? No, that's for race time. For fastest lap time, we have to parse the string. But we can compare as string if format is consistent: "MM:SS.mmm". For example, "1:30.123" is actually "1:30.123" meaning 1 minute, 30.123 seconds. But sometimes it might be "1:30.123" without leading zero for minutes. Comparing strings might not give correct ordering because "1:30.123" vs "2:30.123" would compare incorrectly if we compare strings lexicographically. Better to convert to seconds.

We can convert fastestLapTime to seconds: 
- Extract minutes: substr(fastestLapTime, 1, instr(fastestLapTime, ':')-1)
- Extract seconds and milliseconds: substr(fastestLapTime, instr(fastestLapTime, ':')+1)
- Then convert to seconds: cast(minutes as integer)*60 + cast(seconds_part as real)

But note: fastestLapTime may have format like "1:30.123". So we can do:

WITH times AS (
  SELECT fastestLap,
         fastestLapTime,
         CAST(SUBSTR(fastestLapTime, 1, INSTR(fastestLapTime, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) * 60 +
         CAST(SUBSTR(fastestLapTime, INSTR(fastestLapTime, ':') + 1) AS REAL) AS fastestLapSeconds
  FROM results
  WHERE fastestLapTime IS NOT NULL
)

Then we can find the minimum fastestLapSeconds for the champion in 2009.

Alternatively, we can use the `milliseconds` column? Wait, there is a `milliseconds` column in results, but that's for the total race time, not fastest lap time. There is also `fastestLapSpeed` but not time in milliseconds. So we must parse.

But maybe the question expects just the fastest lap number from the result where the champion set his fastest lap (i.e., the lap number of his fastest lap across the season). So we need to output a single number: the lap number.

Let's construct the query step by step.

First, get champion driverId:

WITH champion AS (
  SELECT ds.driverId
  FROM driverStandings ds
  JOIN races r ON ds.raceId = r.raceId
  WHERE r.year = 2009
    AND ds.position = 1
  ORDER BY r.round DESC
  LIMIT 1
)

This gives the driver who was first in the standings at the last race of 2009.

Then, get the fastest lap number:

SELECT r.fastestLap
FROM results res
JOIN races ra ON res.raceId = ra.raceId
JOIN champion c ON res.driverId = c.driverId
WHERE ra.year = 2009
  AND res.fastestLapTime IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY 
  CAST(SUBSTR(res.fastestLapTime, 1, INSTR(res.fastestLapTime, ':') - 1) AS INTEGER) * 60 +
  CAST(SUBSTR(res.fastestLapTime, INSTR(res.fastestLapTime, ':') + 1) AS REAL)
LIMIT 1;

This will give the fastest lap number for the champion's fastest lap in

WITH RECURSIVE
german_cards AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT c.uuid, c.subtypes, c.supertypes
    FROM cards c
    JOIN foreign_data f ON c.uuid = f.uuid
    WHERE f.language = 'German'
      AND c.subtypes IS NOT NULL
      AND c.supertypes IS NOT NULL
),
split_subtypes AS (
    SELECT uuid,
           substr(subtypes, 1, instr(subtypes || ',', ',') - 1) AS subtype,
           substr(subtypes, instr(subtypes || ',', ',') + 1) AS remainder
    FROM german_cards
    WHERE subtypes != ''
    UNION ALL
    SELECT uuid,
           substr(remainder, 1, instr(remainder || ',', ',') - 1),
           substr(remainder, instr(remainder || ',', ',') + 1)
    FROM split_subtypes
    WHERE remainder != ''
),
split_supertypes AS (
    SELECT uuid,
           substr(supertypes, 1, instr(supertypes || ',', ',') - 1) AS supertype,
           substr(supertypes, instr(supertypes || ',', ',') + 1) AS remainder
    FROM german_cards
    WHERE supertypes != ''
    UNION ALL
    SELECT uuid,
           substr(remainder, 1, instr(remainder || ',', ',') - 1),
           substr(remainder, instr(remainder || ',', ',') + 1)
    FROM split_supertypes
    WHERE remainder != ''
),
all_types AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT subtype AS type
    FROM split_subtypes
    WHERE subtype != ''
    UNION
    SELECT DISTINCT supertype AS type
    FROM split_supertypes
    WHERE supertype != ''
)
SELECT type
FROM all_types
ORDER BY type;

WITH female_clients AS (
    SELECT client_id, birth_date, district_id
    FROM client
    WHERE gender = 'F'
),
oldest_birth_date AS (
    SELECT MIN(birth_date) AS oldest_date
    FROM female_clients
),
oldest_female AS (
    SELECT fc.client_id, fc.birth_date, fc.district_id
    FROM female_clients fc
    INNER JOIN oldest_birth_date obd ON fc.birth_date = obd.oldest_date
),
oldest_female_with_salary AS (
    SELECT ofc.client_id, ofc.birth_date, ofc.district_id, d.A11 AS avg_salary
    FROM oldest_female ofc
    INNER JOIN district d ON ofc.district_id = d.district_id
),
min_salary_among_oldest AS (
    SELECT MIN(avg_salary) AS min_avg_salary
    FROM oldest_female_with_salary
),
oldest_female_lowest_salary AS (
    SELECT ofs.client_id, ofs.birth_date, ofs.district_id, ofs.avg_salary
    FROM oldest_female_with_salary ofs
    INNER JOIN min_salary_among_oldest ms ON ofs.avg_salary = ms.min_avg_salary
),
max_salary_overall AS (
    SELECT MAX(A11) AS max_avg_salary
    FROM district
),
gap_calc AS (
    SELECT 
        (SELECT max_avg_salary FROM max_salary_overall) AS max_salary,
        (SELECT min_avg_salary FROM min_salary_among_oldest) AS min_salary,
        (SELECT max_avg_salary FROM max_salary_overall) - 
        (SELECT min_avg_salary FROM min_salary_among_oldest) AS salary_gap
)
SELECT DISTINCT 
    a.account_id,
    gc.salary_gap
FROM oldest_female_lowest_salary ofls
INNER JOIN disp dp ON ofls.client_id = dp.client_id
INNER JOIN account a ON dp.account_id = a.account_id
CROSS JOIN gap_calc gc;

WITH target_district AS (

    FROM district
    WHERE A3 = 'south Bohemia'
      AND A4 = (SELECT MAX(A4) FROM district WHERE A3 = 'south Bohemia')
),
account_in_target AS (
    SELECT account_id
    FROM account
    WHERE district_id IN (SELECT district_id FROM target_district)
),
owner_clients AS (
    SELECT d.client_id
    FROM disp d
    JOIN account_in_target a ON d.account_id = a.account_id
    WHERE d.type = 'OWNER'
),
client_genders AS (
    SELECT c.gender
    FROM owner_clients oc
    JOIN client c ON oc.client_id = c.client_id
),
counts AS (
    SELECT 
        COUNT(*) AS total_clients,
        SUM(CASE WHEN gender = 'M' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS male_clients
    FROM client_genders
)
SELECT 
    male_clients * 100.0 / total_clients AS male_client_percentage
FROM counts;

WITH male_loans AS (
    SELECT 
        l.loan_id,
        l.amount,
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM l.date) AS loan_year,
        c.gender,
        d.type AS disp_type
    FROM loan l
    JOIN account a ON l.account_id = a.account_id
    JOIN disp d ON a.account_id = d.account_id
    JOIN client c ON d.client_id = c.client_id
    WHERE c.gender = 'M' 
      AND d.type = 'OWNER'
      AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM l.date) IN (1996, 1997)
),
yearly_totals AS (
    SELECT 
        loan_year,
        SUM(amount) AS total_amount
    FROM male_loans
    GROUP BY loan_year
),
pivoted AS (
    SELECT 
        SUM(CASE WHEN loan_year = 1996 THEN total_amount ELSE 0 END) AS sum_1996,
        SUM(CASE WHEN loan_year = 1997 THEN total_amount ELSE 0 END) AS sum_1997
    FROM yearly_totals
)
SELECT 
    sum_1996,
    sum_1997,
    CASE 
        WHEN sum_1996 = 0 THEN NULL 
        ELSE ROUND(((sum_1997 - sum_1996) * 100.0 / sum_1996), 2) 
    END AS growth_rate_percent
FROM pivoted;
